key_insight: Compass improves prostate cancer detection from micro-ultrasound by combining multi-view video context instead of relying on single images alone.
clinical_relevance: This approach could help complement expert reading of micro-ultrasound studies by better capturing 3D prostate context during biopsy assessment.
method: Models a micro-ultrasound study as a stream of 2D frames
Fuses rotational sweep videos with biopsy-time frames
Uses an angle-conditioned transformer to aggregate evidence and output frame-level and study-level risk scores
evidence_signal: The model was trained and evaluated on a multi-center clinical trial dataset of micro-ultrasound studies and compared against published AI baselines and clinical expert risk scores.
watch_item: Performance may depend on consistent acquisition of rotational sweep videos and probe-angle information, which could affect real-world deployment.
paper_title: Compass: Prostate Cancer Detection Needs Multi-View Context
